page: Amazon Web Services u-3tb1.56xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8176M processor with 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing approximately 13.71 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring 169,737.36 in stress-ng multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 9,402.84 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 186.88 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Redis SET operations also perform exceptionally well, achieving 145.62 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is relatively modest, with Geekbench single-core scores falling into the lower tier.

Given its massive memory footprint and high vCPU count, this instance is designed for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The absence of local storage means all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider alongside the 50 Gbps network baseline. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a specialized tier where resource density is heavily skewed toward RAM, making it highly suitable for large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and complex data-compiling tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services u-3tb1.56xlarge is a memory-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor, designed for massive enterprise workloads. It features 224 vCPUs across 112 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8176M processors running at 2.1 GHz, paired with 3072.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark evaluations, it exhibits top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, alongside high-throughput Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance remains average to weak. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing where massive RAM capacity is required, though users must rely entirely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u-3tb1.56xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 224 vCPUs, 3 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large-scale in-memory databases.

tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform featuring three terabytes of RAM and two hundred twenty-four virtual cores for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 3072 GB of DDR4 memory and 224 vCPUs
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps via Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for large in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
